tasks.title: Lap-pin mismatch: warning-first reclassification
tasks.description:
Context: OpenSpec change release-0-10-0-reliability-and-model-routing, design §1, tasks 1.1 / 1.2 / 1.4 / 1.6. Today validatePinnedLap sets lapPinMismatch and routes the mismatch through the failed / issue-worthy path; runOne treats it as a terminal failure. Telemetry is New Relic only (Sentry was removed in 0.9.1).

Outcome: wrong_lap_consumed / multi_lap_consumed become warning-level diagnostics, not operator-worthy failures. Run/try records still capture the mismatch reason in FailReason/metadata, but no FailureCategory is attached and no operator Issue is captured. Mismatch stays a non-retry terminal run result that advances to the next scheduler candidate (existing handoff/next-candidate behavior preserved, retry budget unchanged). A new telemetry.LevelWarning level exists and maps through the active New Relic sink as RallyDiagnostic level=warning.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go (mismatch branch in runOne / validatePinnedLap, issue-worthy guard, preflight guard); internal/telemetry (add LevelWarning + sink mapping). Do not add a new FailureCategory; do not change retry-budget semantics.

Design: keep mismatch outside the FailureCategory taxonomy (0.9.x reserves categories for OutcomeFailed). Keep FailReason / mismatch metadata for audit. Add a run-state preflight guard before/just after mismatch detection: if the pinned lap is already complete elsewhere, mark the run complete and route next rather than escalating. Emit event_kind=lap_pin_mismatch + mismatch_reason=... tags without failure_category (the tag assertions land in the next lap). Wire LevelWarning through the New Relic sink so warnings are never promoted to operator-worthy failures.

Acceptance: go build ./...; existing go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/telemetry/... still pass; the mismatch path no longer reaches issue-worthy capture; LevelWarning is a usable level on the sink. Do not tick tasks.md (VERIFY ticks boxes).

tasks.title: Lap-pin mismatch tests + telemetry assertions
tasks.description:
Context: builds on the warning-first mismatch behavior from the previous lap. Tasks 1.3, 1.5.

Outcome: tests prove a mismatch-only run keeps complete run/try records carrying the mismatch reason but produces no operator-worthy failure capture and no FailureCategory; telemetry tests assert mismatch events carry event_kind=lap_pin_mismatch and mismatch_reason=wrong_lap_consumed|multi_lap_consumed and do NOT carry failure_category or an error-level issue capture.

Files & scope: internal/relay/*_test.go (extend runner_outcome_test.go / runner_telemetry_test.go / runner_failure_telemetry_test.go following existing patterns).

Acceptance: go test ./internal/relay/... passes; the new assertions fail if mismatch ever regains failure-category or issue capture. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Run/role header semantics (run: X/Y + role + model)
tasks.description:
Context: design §2, tasks 3.1-3.4. The non-laps header today renders a bare [N/M] counter and shows the model on a separate line.

Outcome: the non-laps run counter renders run: N/M; the header is role-first with harness/model on the same line (e.g. "VERIFY: codex - g55-xh - started 16:40"), preserving attempts inline. The lap-backed header path (title + laps: X/Y) is unchanged.

Files & scope: internal/style/style.go (add RoleLabel to HeaderOptions; change the non-laps counter format; put model on the role header line), internal/relay/runner.go (header call site passes role via task.Assignee + model context), internal/style/style_test.go.

Acceptance: update style_test.go assertions from [N/M] to run: N/M (3.3); add a snapshot-style assertion that the no-laps header includes role + model on one line (3.4); go test ./internal/style/... ./internal/relay/... passes. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify stage 1 (lap-pin mismatch + run/role headers)
tasks.description:
Use the openspec-verify-change skill against release-0-10-0-reliability-and-model-routing, focused on phase 1 (lap-pin mismatch warning + handoff) and phase 3 (run/role header semantics).

Before diffing, identify the intended merge target (ask the user / PR metadata / repo docs / branch config / recent history — do not assume main) and the first lap/try of this batch; treat earlier branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks to include it. Use git diff <target>...HEAD.

Trace the real lines, not just green tests: confirm the mismatch branch in runner.go no longer reaches issue-worthy / FailureCategory capture yet still records FailReason + mismatch_reason; confirm telemetry.LevelWarning exists and maps to RallyDiagnostic level=warning through the New Relic sink (not Sentry — removed 0.9.1); confirm the preflight guard for an already-complete pinned lap; confirm the header renders run: N/M and a role+model line. Run go build ./... and go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/style/... ./internal/telemetry/....

Check off tasks.md 1.1-1.6 and 3.1-3.4 only for work verified correct and thorough. For substantive gaps, laps add head a focused fix lap rather than fixing here (tiny safe one-liners excepted). Do not rewrite git history.

tasks.title: Cancelled outcome data model (enum + store field)
tasks.description:
Context: design §3, task 2.1. The TryOutcome enum lives in internal/reliability/outcome.go (not records.go, despite the task heading); TryRecord is in internal/store/records.go.

Outcome: a first-class cancelled lifecycle outcome exists. reliability.TryOutcome gains OutcomeCancelled = "cancelled" with its predicate methods updated so cancelled is neither success nor failure: not IsSuccess, not CarriesFailureCategory, not ShouldCaptureIssue, FailureClass = FailureAgent (never infra/freeze), and not terminal-via-failure-category. TryRecord gains CancellationSource string `json:"cancellation_source,omitempty"` with values skip|graceful_stop|quit_now, and cancelled records derive legacy Completed=false for backward compatibility.

Files & scope: internal/reliability/outcome.go, internal/store/records.go. Do NOT add a parallel TryRecord.Status taxonomy — cancelled extends the existing Outcome model (design decision 6). Keep store JSON omitempty so existing fixtures are unaffected.

Acceptance: go build ./...; extend internal/reliability/outcome_test.go and internal/store/store_test.go (or migration_test.go) to cover the cancelled predicates + Completed=false derivation; go test ./internal/reliability/... ./internal/store/... passes. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Runner cancellation plumbing + exit override
tasks.description:
Context: design §3.2, tasks 2.2, 2.3, 2.4, 2.7. Builds on OutcomeCancelled + CancellationSource. This is the highest-risk slice: operator intent must win over process/context cancellation surfacing as a harness error.

Outcome: add a typed cancellation source to actionLoopResult (e.g. CancellationSourceNone|Skip|GracefulStop|QuitNow) instead of inferring from actionTaken/skipFlag/stopFlag. Set skip for Ctrl+S; change Ctrl+X graceful stop to cancel/drain the active attempt, set graceful_stop, then stop the relay; set quit_now for Ctrl+C. After draining tryCh, derive the attempt outcome in this order: explicit operator cancellation → success/finalization → lap-pin mismatch warning → ordinary failure classification.

For cancelled: no "failed: harness error"; cancelled wins even if the subprocess exits cleanly after SIGINT; no failure taxonomy / retry classification; no retry scheduling; no infra/freeze/pause counter increments; no operator-worthy failure capture. Preserve route semantics: Ctrl+S still advances to the next scheduler candidate; graceful stop halts after the cancellation/drain point; quit-now aborts the relay after recording the cancelled attempt. Make post-loop unfinalized-run handling cancellation-aware: cancelled laps-backed attempts must NOT hit the incomplete_finalization failure-capture path, and active run-state cleanup for cancellation must not erase lap/handoff/session fields needed downstream.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go (action loop, runOne outcome derivation, maybeWriteStubAndClearState / post-loop handling). Tests land in the cancelled-tests lap.

Acceptance: go build ./...; go test ./internal/relay/... still passes (full cancelled-scenario tests added later). Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Cancelled downstream consumers + muted styling
tasks.description:
Context: design §3.2 steps 5-6, tasks 2.5, 2.6.

Outcome: every consumer treats cancelled as neither failed nor success. Update tallyRuns, the final relay summary rendering (style.RenderSummary / footer), recent-try context generation, telemetry try-log fields, and failure-capture guards so cancelled records are not counted or displayed as failed. Render the cancelled outcome in muted/grey styling through the style layer (not red failure, not green success); summaries print cancelled rather than failed.

Files & scope: internal/relay/runner.go (tallyRuns, recent-try context, telemetry fields, failure-capture guards), internal/style/style.go (muted cancelled rendering in header/footer/summary).

Acceptance: go build ./...; cancelled appears in muted form in summary/footer output; go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/style/... passes (dedicated assertions added in the cancelled-tests lap). Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Cancelled state test matrix
tasks.description:
Context: task 2.8 — full cancelled test matrix across the prior three cancelled laps.

Outcome: tests cover — Ctrl+S cancelled try persisted as cancelled not failed; Ctrl+X graceful stop cancels/drains, records graceful_stop, and stops the relay; quit-now cancellation overrides harness error handling and records cancelled; cancelled attempts do not retry / freeze / pause or emit failure telemetry; cancelled laps-backed attempts do not emit incomplete_finalization telemetry; cancelled attempts are not counted as failed in summaries/tallies; style tests prove cancelled output uses muted formatting.

Files & scope: internal/relay/*_test.go (runner_action_loop_test.go, runner_tally_test.go, runner_failure_telemetry_test.go, runner_outcome_test.go), internal/style/style_test.go.

Acceptance: go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/style/... passes with an assertion for each scenario above. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify phase 2 (cancelled state for operator exits)
tasks.description:
Use openspec-verify-change against the change, focused on phase 2 (cancelled state for operator-controlled exits) — the highest-risk slice.

Identify the merge target (do not assume main) and the batch's first lap/try; pre-existing branch work stays out of scope unless the user includes it; git diff <target>...HEAD.

Trace real behavior, not just green tests: confirm OutcomeCancelled + CancellationSource are persisted with Completed=false; confirm operator cancellation wins even when the subprocess exits cleanly after SIGINT; confirm no retry/freeze/pause increments, no failure taxonomy, no operator-worthy failure capture, and no incomplete_finalization for cancelled laps-backed attempts; confirm Ctrl+X drains-then-stops and Ctrl+S advances; confirm tallies/summaries/recent-try/telemetry never count cancelled as failed; confirm muted styling (not red/green). Run go test ./internal/relay/... ./internal/store/... ./internal/reliability/... ./internal/style/....

Check off tasks.md 2.1-2.8 only where verified correct and thorough; head-lap any substantive gap; no history rewrites.

tasks.title: Reasoning config schema + role resolver + propagation
tasks.description:
Context: design §4, tasks 4.1, 4.2, 4.3.

Outcome: a [reasoning] table maps role name (case-insensitive) → harness-aware reasoning/model preference, resolved only after the route-selected harness is known (never as a global alias). A resolver helper applies role-level model-alias fallback after ActiveRoute(task.Assignee) selects the route, only when the entry has no explicit model token, preserving explicit route models at higher precedence and resolving to a named model alias in the selected harness. Resolved route/runtime data carries optional {model, reasoning_effort} while preserving model-alias-only behavior.

Files & scope: internal/config/config_v2.go + rawConfig ([reasoning] map[string]string), internal/routing (resolver helper), and propagation through agent.ResolvedAgent, agent.RunOptions, relay.Config.Resolver call sites, and routeSelection. Keep existing [routes], [defaults.*_model], and [harness.<name>.models] behavior unchanged.

Design: lane-aware fallback fires only when task role + selected harness are both known and the route entry has no explicit model. Return both the selected model and the optional effort so each executor can inject via its own mechanism (executor wiring is the next lap).

Acceptance: go build ./...; existing config/routing tests pass; add resolver unit tests for precedence (explicit model wins; role alias resolves to a harness model alias). go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/routing/... ./internal/relay/.... Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Harness-specific effort injection + validation tests
tasks.description:
Context: design §4.1/4.2, tasks 4.4, 4.5. Builds on the resolved {model, reasoning_effort}.

Outcome: executor argument builders inject harness-specific effort — codex: -c model_reasoning_effort=<value>; claude: --effort <value>; opencode: --variant <value>; gemini: warn and skip; antigravity: resolve effort via model aliases/model names only. Validation: a missing harness-scoped model alias is a clear rally routes check error (likely an operator typo); an unknown/unsupported effort value warns rather than hard-fails; an unsupported-harness effort warns and skips injection.

Files & scope: internal/agent/<harness>.go argument builders (codex, claude, opencode, gemini, antigravity), the routes-check validation path, and their tests.

Acceptance: tests for — missing harness-scoped model alias → routes check error; route explicit model still wins; role alias resolves to an existing harness model alias; unknown unsupported effort warns not fails; unsupported harness effort warns + skips. go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/rally/.... Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Document reasoning variants (config.toml + README)
tasks.description:
Context: task 4.6.

Outcome: the .rally/config.toml sample and the README routing/model sections document role-variant examples (verify = "g55-xh", junior = "g55-l") and the harness-specific effort notes (codex -c model_reasoning_effort, claude --effort, opencode --variant, gemini unsupported, antigravity model-alias).

Files & scope: .rally/config.toml (sample [reasoning] block), README.md (routing/model section). Docs only — keep examples consistent with the implemented schema and precedence.

Acceptance: examples match the implemented behavior from the two prior reasoning laps; README reads cleanly. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify phase 4 (reasoning variant aliases)
tasks.description:
Use openspec-verify-change against the change, focused on phase 4 (reasoning variant aliases and role resolution) — a shared routing/model contract.

Identify the merge target (not assumed main) and the batch start; git diff <target>...HEAD.

Trace: confirm [reasoning] resolves only after harness selection (no global-alias leakage), explicit route models keep precedence, and the resolver returns {model, reasoning_effort}; confirm each executor injects/skips correctly (codex -c, claude --effort, opencode --variant, gemini warn/skip, antigravity model-alias); confirm rally routes check errors on a missing harness-scoped alias and warns (not fails) on unknown/unsupported effort; confirm the config.toml + README examples match behavior. go test ./internal/config/... ./internal/routing/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/rally/....

Check off tasks.md 4.1-4.6 where verified; head-lap gaps; no history rewrites.

tasks.title: Fix reasoning-resolved resilience keys
tasks.description: Phase 4 verify found that routeRuntime applies [reasoning] after route selection, but several resilience helpers still derive keys from state.Entry.Spec before role reasoning is applied. This breaks harness-scoped model aliases such as verify=g55-xh: bench/pause/probation and quota fan-out consult codex:<base-model> while runOne records state for codex:<resolved-variant-model>. Update routeRuntime syncRecoverySignals, selectionWaitError, forceUnpauseAll, and benchQuotaScope to derive keys from the role-resolved selection for the relevant lane/assignee, then add regression coverage proving a reasoning-selected model stays benched/paused until its real resilience key clears.

tasks.title: rally tail syntax highlighting (--highlight)
tasks.description:
Context: design §5.2, tasks 5.1-5.4.

Outcome: rally tail gains --highlight off|heuristic|chroma (default off). heuristic adds token-aware coloring (errors, JSON-like lines, timestamps, commands, URLs) with no new dependency. chroma offers richer coloring behind a dependency, gated by --highlight=chroma. Plain (off) mode stays byte-for-byte deterministic.

Files & scope: cmd/rally/tail.go (flag + output loop), a small heuristic highlighter helper, and the optional chroma path. Default off preserves current output exactly.

Acceptance: --highlight=off output is unchanged; heuristic colorizes without a new required dependency; chroma is gated behind its flag/dependency. go test ./cmd/rally/... (tail_test.go). Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: tail active-try run-state metadata + field-wise cleanup
tasks.description:
Context: design §5.1/5.3, tasks 6.1, 6.2. This is the subtle half of active-try tail targeting.

Outcome: progress run-state carries active try metadata — active_relay_id, active_run_id, active_try_id, active_log_path, active_started_at — written at try start before the executor runs and cleared after the try is appended to the store, but only the active-* fields are cleared, via a helper that preserves RunID, PinnedLapID, RecordedLaps, LapsAttempted, HandoffState, and SessionID. Active-tail cleanup must NOT call progress.ClearRunState.

Files & scope: the progress run-state struct + helpers (internal/store or progress package), internal/relay/runner.go (write at try start, field-wise clear after append).

Design: the risk is erasing run/lap/handoff/session fields needed by follow-up handling. Add a dedicated field-wise clear helper; never reuse ClearRunState here.

Acceptance: go build ./...; a unit test proves the clear helper preserves RunID/PinnedLapID/RecordedLaps/LapsAttempted/HandoffState/SessionID while dropping the active-* fields. go test ./internal/store/... ./internal/relay/.... Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: tailTarget active selection + stale fallback + tests
tasks.description:
Context: design §5.1, tasks 6.3, 6.4, 6.5. Builds on the active metadata from the prior lap.

Outcome: in tailTarget, --try 0 uses the active run's active try log when active metadata exists; with no active metadata, fall back to the newest completed try; explicit historical --try N keeps its 1-based semantics. Stale/missing active files (missing log path, implausibly old active_started_at, or metadata belonging to no unfinished relay/run) produce a warning and fall back to the newest completed try.

Files & scope: cmd/rally/tail.go (tailTarget), cmd/rally/tail_test.go.

Acceptance: tests — a fresh workspace with active metadata tails the active log instead of erroring; active metadata beats an older completed try; a stale/missing active path falls back with a warning; stale active metadata from a crashed/finished relay is ignored; historical --try N remains 1-based and unchanged. go test ./cmd/rally/.... Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify stage 4 (tail highlighting + active targeting)
tasks.description:
Use openspec-verify-change against the change, focused on phase 5 (tail syntax highlighting) and phase 6 (tail active-try targeting).

Identify the merge target (not assumed main) and the batch start; git diff <target>...HEAD.

Trace: confirm --highlight default off is byte-identical to prior output and heuristic/chroma behave; confirm active try metadata is written at try start and cleared field-wise (RunID/PinnedLapID/RecordedLaps/LapsAttempted/HandoffState/SessionID preserved, ClearRunState not called); confirm --try 0 picks active over newest-completed, falls back with a warning on stale/missing, and historical --try N stays 1-based. Smoke-check that rally tail on a fresh workspace does not error when only an active try exists. go test ./cmd/rally/... ./internal/store/... ./internal/relay/....

Check off tasks.md 5.1-5.4 and 6.1-6.5 where verified; head-lap gaps; no history rewrites.

tasks.title: opencode usage-limit classification + reset parsing
tasks.description:
Context: design §6, tasks 8.1, 8.2; spike-2 finding A is resolved (8.5 already done). Telemetry is New Relic (Sentry removed 0.9.1).

Outcome: reliability.ParseOpencodeError classifies subscription-provider usage limits as usage_limit. Match across opencode's AI_APICallError / AI_RetryError / UnknownError wrappers, checking error.name, error.data.message, AND the flat server-log error.error="<Wrapper>: <message>" value (confirmed third-pass: the structured error reaches only the server log as this flat field, never a nested data.message on stdout). Add substrings "usage limit reached", "monthly usage limit", "usage limit reached for"; keep usage_limit winning over short_rate_limit (preserve the existing priority test). Add opencode-specific reset parsing (do NOT overload the gemini regex): space-separated spans via (\d+)\s+(day|hour|minute|second)s? ("Resets in 7 days" / "... 5 hour" / "... 30 minutes") and absolute timestamps ("reset at" / "will reset at 2026-06-16 18:29:51") with NO timezone marker — parse in time.Local (layout 2006-01-02 15:04:05), treat as approximate (benching slightly long is the safe direction). Feed FailureEvidence.ResetAfter / ResetAt so benchResetDeadline uses the real reset instead of BenchDefaultDuration.

Files & scope: internal/reliability/opencode.go (+ a reset-parse helper). Server-log-tail evidence sourcing is the next lap.

Acceptance: go build ./...; go test ./internal/reliability/... passes including the usage_limit-over-short_rate_limit priority test. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: opencode server-log-tail evidence + session correlation
tasks.description:
Context: design §6.1.3, task 8.3 (finding A). Highest-risk part of phase 8: a new external-log read plus session correlation, because opencode retries provider errors internally and emits nothing to the JSON stream during the stall.

Outcome: when an opencode try ends without a usable result (stall kill or error event), OpenCodeExecutor reads the tail of opencode's server log (~/.local/share/opencode/log/opencode.log — only this file carries message="stream error" lines; the per-run timestamped *.log files do not) and surfaces any usage-limit signature as FailureEvidence. Correlate the session WITHOUT relying on stdout (empty during the stall): match message=created ... directory=<WorkspaceDir> to recover session.id=, then scan that session's level=ERROR message="stream error" lines; fall back to providerID=<provider> (the prefix of the --model arg) within the try's wall-clock window, and narrow by --session id when already known (resumed runs). Match either agent=build (small=false) or agent=title (small=true) — both carry the limit text. Prefer this over inferring usage-limit from a silent stall.

Files & scope: internal/agent/opencode.go (server-log tail read + correlation, emit FailureEvidence). No bench-side change is needed — routing.QuotaScope already keys opencode:zai-coding-plan / opencode:opencode-go and the runner benches on Category == CategoryUsageLimit. Reuse the prior lap's matchers/reset-parse on the recovered text.

Design: bound the tail read and the wall-clock window; degrade gracefully if opencode.log is absent.

Acceptance: go build ./...; go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/reliability/... (full evidence/correlation tests in the next lap). Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: opencode usage-limit test matrix
tasks.description:
Context: task 8.4 — test matrix across the two prior opencode laps.

Outcome: tests cover — opencode fixtures for the exact zai ("Usage limit reached for 5 hour. Your limit will reset at <ts>") and opencode-go ("Monthly usage limit reached. Resets in 7 days.") signatures in the flat error.error="<Wrapper>: ..." server-log form, for both AI_APICallError and AI_RetryError wrappers, asserting CategoryUsageLimit + correct ResetAfter/ResetAt; the UnknownError generic-message + server-log-tail evidence path classifies usage_limit; server-log-tail session correlation by directory=<WorkspaceDir> (and the provider+window fallback) picks the right session's limit line; a quota-scope bench is triggered for opencode:zai-coding-plan / opencode:opencode-go on these failures (not agent_error).

Files & scope: internal/reliability/opencode_test.go (+ fixtures), internal/agent/*_test.go, and a relay-level bench assertion where appropriate.

Acceptance: go test ./internal/reliability/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/relay/... passes with each scenario above. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Verify phase 8 (opencode usage-limit detection)
tasks.description:
Use openspec-verify-change against the change, focused on phase 8 (opencode usage-limit detection + reset parsing + server-log-tail evidence) — a production failure-classification path that gates quota benching.

Identify the merge target (not assumed main) and the batch start; git diff <target>...HEAD.

Trace real behavior: confirm ParseOpencodeError matches the flat server-log error.error="<Wrapper>: <message>" form across AI_APICallError / AI_RetryError / UnknownError and that usage_limit beats short_rate_limit; confirm reset parsing handles both spans and the timezone-less absolute timestamp in time.Local without overloading the gemini regex; confirm the server-log-tail path correlates the session by directory and falls back by provider+window, reads only opencode.log, and emits FailureEvidence despite an empty-stdout stall; confirm CategoryUsageLimit benches opencode:zai-coding-plan / opencode:opencode-go (not agent_error). Note 8.5 is already resolved/checked — confirm it is correctly marked. go test ./internal/reliability/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/relay/....

Check off tasks.md 8.1-8.4 where verified; head-lap gaps; no history rewrites.

tasks.title: Release notes + checklist + short rate-limit assertion
tasks.description:
Context: design §7, tasks 7.1, 7.2, 7.3, 8.6. Per AGENTS.md, the historical RALLY-* incident IDs are legacy Sentry references that cannot be queried in New Relic — cite them as historical only and describe 0.10.0 behavior in backend-neutral / New Relic terms.

Outcome: short rate-limit stays a non-error (info) signal retaining event_kind=limit_signal + failure_category=short_rate_limit (not reclassified as a crash/failure) — add/confirm a small assertion (7.1). Release notes list the exact historical incident IDs: the 7.2 set (RALLY-2, RALLY-3, RALLY-4, RALLY-6, RALLY-8, RALLY-9, RALLY-B, RALLY-C) and the opencode set 8.6 (RALLY-Q, RALLY-K, RALLY-D), described in New Relic / backend-neutral terms. Capture the release checklist verification (7.3): no alert regression for routine rate-limit categories, corrected run header text, muted cancelled output, and rally tail defaulting to active output.

Files & scope: the changelog / release-notes doc, README if it hosts the checklist, and a reliability test for the short-rate-limit tags if not already covered.

Acceptance: incident IDs are present and labeled historical; the checklist is captured; go test ./internal/reliability/... for the short-rate-limit assertion. Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Bump VERSION to 0.10.0
tasks.description:
Context: design decision 7, tasks 9.1, 9.2. NOTE: the task text says "from 0.9.0", but the working tree is already at 0.9.3 — bump the current value to 0.10.0 regardless of the stale starting number in the task.

Outcome: internal/buildinfo/VERSION reads 0.10.0. main.Version stays "dev" in source (GoReleaser injects the real version via ldflags at build time; the auto-tag workflow creates the vX.Y.Z tag from VERSION on main — do not tag by hand and do not edit main.Version).

Files & scope: internal/buildinfo/VERSION only (confirm main.Version is unchanged in source).

Acceptance: VERSION reads 0.10.0; go build ./... reports a v0.10.0-dev dev build; main.Version is still "dev". Do not tick tasks.md.

tasks.title: Final full-change verification (0.10.0 release)
tasks.description:
Run the complete openspec-verify-change against release-0-10-0-reliability-and-model-routing and inspect the whole diff.

Identify the intended merge target from the user / PR metadata / repo docs / branch config / recent history (do not assume main) and the first lap/try of this batch; treat pre-batch branch work as pre-existing unless the user asks to include it. Use git diff <target>...HEAD across the full change.

Verify the whole outcome end-to-end, tracing actual symbols/strings/commits, not just green tests: mismatch warning-first + handoff (phase 1), run/role headers (phase 3), cancelled state with muted output and no false failures (phase 2), reasoning aliases + harness effort injection (phase 4), tail highlighting (phase 5) + active-try targeting (phase 6), short-rate-limit / telemetry closure + release notes (phase 7), opencode usage-limit detection + server-log-tail (phase 8), and VERSION = 0.10.0 with main.Version still "dev" (phase 9). Run go build ./..., the full go test ./..., and go vet ./...; do realistic smoke checks where feasible (rally tail active-run, run header text, cancelled muted output).

Confirm every tasks.md box that is genuinely done-and-verified is checked (including the phase-VERIFY boxes and the already-resolved 8.5); for anything incomplete or wrong, laps add head a focused fix lap rather than implementing here (tiny safe one-liners excepted). Do not rewrite git history; if scope cleanup is needed, use additive/revert commits or ask the user for a recovery strategy.
